title: Function value nan while computing limit

posts.name: Saswati Nandan
posts.text:
Dear experts,
I am trying to calculate limit using the following command
combine --method AsymptoticLimits datacard
but getting this error
[#0] ERROR:Eval -- RooAbsReal::logEvalError(systeff_HH_resolved_1b_nonvbf_TT_erdON) evaluation error,  origin       : AsymPow::systeff_HH_resolved_1b_nonvbf_TT_erdON[ kappaLow=0.000000 kappaHigh=0.000000 theta=erdON ]  message      : function value is NAN  server values: kappaLow=0.000000=0, kappaHigh=0.000000=0, theta=erdON=0 +/- 1 [#0] ERROR:Eval -- RooAbsReal::logEvalError(n_exp_final_binHH_resolved_1b_nonvbf_proc_TT) evaluation error,
Didn’t see this error before. I put the datacard here
/afs/cern.ch/user/s/snandan/public/datacard

Did anybody face this error before? It would be nice if someone tell what is going wrong.
Thanks and Regards,
Saswati
posts.cooked_html:
Dear experts,
I am trying to calculate limit using the following command

combine --method AsymptoticLimits datacard

but getting this error
[#0] ERROR:Eval -- RooAbsReal::logEvalError(systeff_HH_resolved_1b_nonvbf_TT_erdON) evaluation error, origin : AsymPow::systeff_HH_resolved_1b_nonvbf_TT_erdON[ kappaLow=0.000000 kappaHigh=0.000000 theta=erdON ] message : function value is NAN server values: kappaLow=0.000000=0, kappaHigh=0.000000=0, theta=erdON=0 +/- 1 [#0] ERROR:Eval -- RooAbsReal::logEvalError(n_exp_final_binHH_resolved_1b_nonvbf_proc_TT) evaluation error,

Didn’t see this error before. I put the datacard here

/afs/cern.ch/user/s/snandan/public/datacard

Did anybody face this error before? It would be nice if someone tell what is going wrong.

Thanks and Regards,
Saswati

posts.name: Andrew Gilbert
posts.text:
Hi Saswati,
It looks like you have a shape uncertainty erdON for process TT in channel HH_resolved_1b_nonvbf where the Up and Down histograms are empty. This is not something combine can handle, so it’s worth a look if the histograms are being filled correctly, and if this is just an issue of very low statistics for a small background, consider whether the shape uncertainty needs to be applied here.
cheers,
Andrew
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Saswati,

It looks like you have a shape uncertainty erdON for process TT in channel HH_resolved_1b_nonvbf where the Up and Down histograms are empty. This is not something combine can handle, so it’s worth a look if the histograms are being filled correctly, and if this is just an issue of very low statistics for a small background, consider whether the shape uncertainty needs to be applied here.

cheers,
Andrew
